description:
Turn `/bw workers` into a real worker operations surface and keep its code lane separate from the issue explorer.

Scope:
- implement the Workers tab/overlay using the existing worker diagnostics model
- group workers by active run/epic when metadata exists
- keep manually delegated/background workers visible separately
- support detail views for log/tmux/runtime paths
- add state-aware land/cancel actions
- add manual cleanup for landed workers when cleanup policy is `keep`
- keep action enable/disable rules explicit and well-tested

Primary file lane:
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/tui/worker-manager.ts`
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/actions/workers.ts`
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/actions/landing.ts`
- `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/actions/cleanup.ts`
- worker-manager-focused tests under `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/__tests__`

Acceptance criteria:
- `/bw:workers` and the dashboard worker surface show grouped worker state clearly
- land/cancel actions are available only in valid states
- cleanup is available only for valid landed/verified workers
- detail views expose the paths/operators need for follow-up
- tests cover grouping, action guards, and cleanup restrictions

Parallel-safety note:
- after the scaffolding ticket lands, this ticket should be safe to run in parallel with the issue-explorer lane as long as it stays out of issue-browser files and avoids broad rewrites of `dashboard.ts`.

comments.text: Recovering ticket after worker exited but landing/remediation stalled. Taking over manually to land the existing worker commit cleanly onto feat/bw-tui without disturbing the still-running sbdpi-wx1.4 worker.

title: Implement the worker manager overlay and shared worker action lane
